note: Strength-of-schedule tilt layer (v2.3 engine). Per NFL offense, positional schedule-ease scores: POSITIVE = easier season-long matchups (faces weaker defenses) -> small boost; NEGATIVE = tougher schedule -> small ding. content.js clamps each value to +/-1.5 and adds it to the recommendation weight as a tiebreaker; it never overrides VBD. Applies to RB/WR/TE.
method: Built from REAL ESPN data (pulled 2026-06-18 via the logged-in browser): each team's 2026 opponents (proTeamSchedules) vs each defense's projected strength (D/ST projection under THIS league's scoring), averaged across the season with the fantasy-championship weeks (NFL 15/16/17) weighted heavier (x2/x2/x3). Scaled so the most extreme schedule maps to the +/-1.5 cap. v1 uses overall defensive strength for all skill positions (not yet split into run-defense vs pass-defense); refine to position-specific later if desired.
weighting: tiebreaker only; cap +/-1.5; positions RB/WR/TE; championship weeks weighted
